title: The Milkman's Son
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: In 1980s Dublin, the son of a milkman navigates poverty, faith, and first love while his father makes deliveries in a city on the edge of change.
long_description: Set in the Liberties of 1980s Dublin, The Milkman's Son follows Declan O'Brien through one formative year as he grapples with the shame of his family's poverty, the weight of Catholic guilt, and the unraveling of his parents' marriage. His father, known only as Da, is the milkman — a man of worn hands and few words who makes deliveries before dawn in a horse-drawn milk float through a city caught between tradition and modernity. When a new girl named Siobhan arrives in his English class, Declan is forced to confront the lies he tells himself and others about who he is and where he comes from. Told in spare, literary prose, the novel traces Declan's slow, painful understanding that his father's quiet dignity is not weakness, and that leaving home requires understanding what you carry with you. Through the long winter of a recession-haunted Dublin, through illness and loss and first love, The Milkman's Son is a story about the work of becoming yourself.
main_characters: Declan O'Brien — a fifteen-year-old scholarship student, sharp and silent, ashamed of his father's work
Da (Matthew O'Brien) — the milkman, worn hands, few words, a man who left school at fourteen
Siobhan Rafferty — the new girl from Galway, bright and unafraid, who reads novels Declan has never heard of
Mam (Bridget O'Brien) — thin, tired, chain-smokes, married too young, regrets too much
Jimmy Malone — Da's helper, a simple man with a kind heart and a photograph he still carries
Brother Finbar — Christian Brother, sharp ruler, sharper suspicion of boys who daydream
Mrs. Cronin — Da's old teacher, an invalid in a Georgian house who leaves tea out for the milkman
Mr. Rafferty — Siobhan's father, an engineer at Intel, polite and ambitious for his daughter
Nellie — The aging mare who pulls the milk float, patient and warm, the one Declan tells everything
